2016-08-01.title: Where CPR on a Boy Is Time Wasted: U.S. Doctors Recall Aleppo’s Horrors

2016-08-24.abstract: A look at earthquakes in Italy that have caused extensive damage over the last century.

2016-08-25.title: Support for Saudi Arabia Gives U.S. Direct Role in Yemen Conflict
2016-08-25.abstract: Saudi-led bombings of medical facilities and schools have caused Doctors Without Borders to scale back its efforts, and have caused Congress to put the Obama administration on notice.

2016-08-25.title: French ‘Burkini’ Bans Provoke Backlash as Armed Police Confront Beachgoers
2016-08-25.abstract: Some politicians began backpedaling as an outcry grew on social media over the apparent public humiliation and ostracism of Muslim women dressed in modest attire.

2016-08-25.title: Kim Jong-un Hails Firing of Submarine Missile as ‘Greatest Success’
2016-08-25.abstract: The North Korean leader claimed that the continental United States and American military bases in the Pacific were now within striking range.

2016-08-25.title: Syria Used Chlorine in Bombs Against Civilians, Report Says
2016-08-25.abstract: A United Nations investigation says that Syrian military helicopters had used the chemical on at least two occasions, and that ISIS had deployed sulfur mustard, which is poisonous.

2016-08-25.title: State Dept. Study on Saudi Textbooks
2016-08-25.abstract: A two-part study of Saudi textbooks commissioned by the State Department and conducted by the International Center for Religion and Diplomacy, a Washington research group, was finished in 2013 but was never released for fear of angering Saudi officials. The New York Times obtained a copy under the Freedom of Information Act.

2016-08-26.title: Besieged Town of Daraya Agrees to Surrender to Syrian Government
2016-08-26.abstract: As the rebel-held town planned for the evacuation of its 8,000 remaining residents, a bombing that killed 14 in Aleppo served as a reminder of what Daraya had faced for four years.

2016-08-26.title: At Least 7 Dead as Shabab Militants Attack Restaurant in Somali Capital
2016-08-26.abstract: The Shabab have carried out a series of deadly attacks in Somalia to try to topple the Western-backed government of the country.

2016-08-26.title: Ex-Military Officers Convicted of Human Rights Crimes During Argentina Dictatorship
2016-08-26.abstract: The 38 former officials were tried in the kidnapping, torture and killing of several hundred victims under a government in power from 1976 to 1983.

2016-08-26.title: Brazilian Police Recommend Charges Against Ryan Lochte
2016-08-26.abstract: The American swimmer could be charged with giving false testimony in connection with claims that he was robbed at gunpoint during the Rio Olympics.

2016-08-26.title: Ai Weiwei Planned to Sculpt a ‘Redline.’ Chinese Censors Say He Crossed One.
2016-08-26.abstract: An invitation to exhibit next month at a show in northwest China was withdrawn, leading the outspoken Mr. Ai to denounce efforts to limit free expression.

2016-08-27.title: Federal Investigators in Brazil to Seek Graft Charges Against Ex-President Da Silva
2016-08-27.abstract: The move by the Federal Police, an investigative force similar to the F.B.I., deals a new blow to Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and his once-dominant Workers’ Party.

2016-08-27.title: Quake Exposes Italy’s Challenge to Retrofit Its Architecture
2016-08-27.abstract: This week’s death and destruction was amplified by the lack of anti-seismic codes that are too costly, too complicated and too cumbersome to approve.

2016-08-27.title: SEAL Team 6 and a Man Left for Dead: A Grainy Picture of Valor
2016-08-27.abstract: An airman with the unit is being considered for the Medal of Honor after new video analysis suggested that he fought alone bravely in a 2002 battle on an Afghan peak.

2016-08-27.title: An Afghan Feud Reignites, Putting Police Families at Odds
2016-08-27.abstract: Prominent northern families are contesting a new killing, fueling a type of infighting that threatens government stability and the fight against the Taliban.

2016-08-27.title: From Bikinis to Burkinis, Regulating What Women Wear
2016-08-27.abstract: What is it about women’s swimwear and general attire that has repeatedly compelled societies to legislate or regulate women’s choices?

2016-08-28.title: A Powerful Russian Weapon: The Spread of False Stories
2016-08-28.abstract: Using both conventional media and covert channels, the Kremlin relies on disinformation to create doubt, fear and discord in Europe and the United States.

2016-08-28.title: 1966: China Admonishes Red Guards
2016-08-28.abstract: From the archives of the International Herald Tribune: Alarmed by growing chaos, the Chinese leadership admonishes Red Guards in August 1966.

2016-08-28.title: 1916: Romania Declares War on Austria-Hungary
2016-08-28.abstract: From the archives of the International Herald Tribune: Romania ends its neutrality and declares war on Austria-Hungary in August 1916.

2016-08-28.title: An Islamic Radical Who Changed His Mind
2016-08-28.abstract: For years, Jesse Morton advocated a violent vision of Islam, inspiring recruits to commit acts of terror. He explained why he recanted and joined a think tank that researches radicalization.

2016-08-28.title: 3 Suspects in 2009 Attack on Sri Lankan Cricket Team Are Killed in Pakistan
2016-08-28.abstract: The police said the men, and a fourth militant, had died in a shootout when another group of militants tried to free them from custody.

2016-08-29.title: Israel Joins Bikini Fray, Ordering Concert Singer to Cover Up
2016-08-29.abstract: In a twist on the French debate over the full-body burkini worn by some Muslim women, Israel is insisting on modest dress by performers at government-sponsored events.

2016-08-29.title: In Syria, Rebels Threaten Kurdish-Controlled Territory as U.S. Allies Clash
2016-08-29.abstract: Aided by Turkish airstrikes, Syrian rebels pressed deeper into the northern part of the country, putting two American-backed forces in conflict.

2016-08-29.title: Australian Aid Worker Who Was Abducted in Afghanistan Is Released
2016-08-29.abstract: Kerry Jane Wilson, who is in her 60s, had been working in the country for about 20 years when she was taken from her office in Jalalabad on April 28.

2016-08-29.title: If You Miss a Train in France, You May Catch a Tune Instead
2016-08-29.abstract: About 100 pianos sit in train stations across France, free for anyone to sit and play, and travelers from all walks of life have taken to doing just that.

2016-08-29.title: Suicide Attack Kills Scores of Military Recruits in Aden, Yemen
2016-08-29.abstract: The Islamic State called the car bombing, in the crowded Mansoura neighborhood, a “martyrdom operation.”

2016-08-30.title: Mexico’s Police Commissioner, Enrique Galindo, Is Fired
2016-08-30.abstract: Mexico’s president dismissed his federal police chief after a report that the police had summarily killed at least 22 suspects in a raid last year on a drug gang hide-out.

2016-08-30.title: Israel Quietly Legalizes Pirate Outposts in the West Bank
2016-08-30.abstract: Unauthorized settlements dot hilltops in the West Bank, and anti-settlement groups and Palestinians say retroactively legalizing them is a methodical effort to change the region’s map.

2016-08-30.title: Corpses, Pythons, Sleep Deprivation: Meditation Rituals in Thailand Can Be Intense
2016-08-30.abstract: At some Buddhist temples, methods include meditating beside a dead body, to learn about selflessness, or in total darkness, to enhance concentration.

2016-08-30.title: Spain’s Interim Leader Bids to Form New Government
2016-08-30.abstract: Mariano Rajoy, the caretaker prime minister, is trying to end the deadlock that has left Spain without an elected government since December.

2016-08-30.title: American Is Facing Spy Trial in China, and Husband Seeks Obama’s Help
2016-08-30.abstract: Phan Phan-Gillis, a Houston businesswoman, was detained last year on allegations dating back 20 years. President Obama is being urged to bring up the case.

2016-08-31.title: Does Killing Terrorist Leaders Make Any Difference? Scholars Are Doubtful
2016-08-31.abstract: Terrorist organizations have features that make them resilient in the face of a senior officer’s death, researchers have found.

2016-08-31.title: Japanese Government Urges Another Increase in Military Spending
2016-08-31.abstract: Missile defenses are a major focus amid challenges from North Korea and China. But the budget proposal is a test of Japan’s commitment to pacifism.

2016-08-31.title: North Korea Has Executed a Deputy Premier, Seoul Reports
2016-08-31.abstract: The North Korean leader, Kim Jong-un, has executed the deputy premier for education, Kim Yong-jin, and purged two other senior officials, South Korea said.

2016-08-31.title: France’s Burkini Debate Reverberates Around the World
2016-08-31.abstract: In other countries, the body-covering swimwear has been embraced as a symbol of modesty, liberation and even diversity.

2016-08-31.title: Scheduled Flights to Cuba From U.S. Begin Again, Now With Jet Engines
2016-08-31.abstract: The last time that an airline in the United States had a regularly scheduled flight to the island, the passengers flew on a propeller plane.
